name: David Montgomery
headline: Goal-line hammer lands in Houston as a value RB2
body:
The film backs the volume case: across three Detroit seasons Montgomery converted 33 rushing touchdowns on heavy red-zone usage, including 36 red-zone opportunities and 16 goal-line carries in 2025 even while ceding the early-down lead role. He still ran efficiently at 4.53 yards per carry on 158 attempts with a 9.49% explosive-run rate, and his 2.11 yards after contact per attempt confirm he finishes runs through traffic. The slim 37.1% snap share and shrinking 5.3% target rate were a product of a committee, not decline.

A trade to Houston resets that ceiling. With only Woody Marks behind him and a rebuilt offensive line fronting a run-leaning attack, Montgomery profiles as the primary back and near-exclusive goal-line option. That touchdown equity, paired with usable receiving chops (24 catches in 2025), makes him a sturdy RB2 with weekly RB1 spike weeks. At an ADP outside the top 50, the value is obvious.
